summary: The implementation successfully covers all five requirements (REQ-14 through REQ-18) for the customer management feature. The backend adds Customer CRUD operations with session protection, the frontend creates CustomerForm and CustomerList components following existing patterns, and React Query hooks are properly implemented. However, there is one notable deviation: the backend Customer type uses 'contactInfo' as a single JSON string field instead of separate fields for mobile, email, referenceSource, and preferences as specified in the BuildRequest.

missing_requirements.requirement: Customer type includes separate fields for mobile, email, referenceSource, preferences
missing_requirements.reason: The backend Customer type uses a single 'contactInfo: Text' field to store JSON-encoded contact data, rather than individual fields as specified. While functionally workable, this deviates from the explicit requirement that the Customer type should have separate fields.

hallucinated_features.description: Customer active status tracking with isActive, createdAt, and updatedAt fields
hallucinated_features.reason: The Customer type includes isActive (boolean), createdAt, and updatedAt (timestamp) fields that were not requested in any requirement. The BuildRequest only specified: id, name, mobile, email, referenceSource, preferences, and address.

hallucinated_features.description: Active vs total customer statistics display
hallucinated_features.reason: CustomersPage displays two stat cards showing 'Total Customers' and 'Active Customers' counts. The BuildRequest did not request customer analytics, reporting, or activity status tracking (explicitly listed as non-goals).
